# First-Agent

> Locally orchestrated, mixed-tier LLM coding agent for a single
> power-user. Filesystem-canonical knowledge (Markdown + YAML
> frontmatter); read-side scales lazily from grep to BM25.
> Written in Python; target codebases the agent reads can be anything.
>
> This file is an index for LLM agents that want to ingest the
> project documentation in one fetch. Paths below are relative
> to the repository root.
>
> **Project stage.** Currently in **Stage 1** (documentation +
> agent development via Devin) of the three-stage evolution defined
> in [knowledge/project-overview.md](./knowledge/project-overview.md).

> **Tiered layout.** §MUST READ FIRST is the smallest path that
> bootstraps a session (five files, in order). §TASK ROUTING
> points task-types at the right primary entrypoint. §BY-DEMAND
> INDEX below is the full per-folder file list — open only the
> entries relevant to the task at hand.

## § MUST READ FIRST

Five files cover ~90% of session-start context. Read in order:

1. [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) — universal session loadout: repo navigation, style, §Pre-flight checklist, §Context-budget discipline, cross-project anti-patterns, §Loadable skills, query routing.
2. [knowledge/project-overview.md](./knowledge/project-overview.md) — §1.1 four-pillar goal; §1.2 minimalism-first principle; §1.2.5 compliance-by-construction.
3. [HANDOFF.md](./HANDOFF.md) — current state tables (Landmarks / Gotchas / Backlog), §Next priority list, §Session Protocol.
4. [knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md](./knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md) — one-paragraph cheat-sheet per ADR + amendments. Authoritative source remains the per-ADR files.
5. [knowledge/glossary.md](./knowledge/glossary.md) — canonical definitions for project-specific terms (axis, lens, pillar, NLAH, ACI, harness, hook, UC1..UC5, MCP, subtraction-first, minimalism-first, R-S-M).

## § TASK ROUTING

Task type → primary entrypoint, then secondary:

- **Inner-loop / tool-registry contract** → [knowledge/adr/ADR-7-inner-loop-tool-registry.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-7-inner-loop-tool-registry.md) (canonical). Background: [knowledge/research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md) §0.
- **New ADR or amendment** → [knowledge/adr/ADR-template.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-template.md) + [knowledge/skills/pr-creation/SKILL.md](./knowledge/skills/pr-creation/SKILL.md) (must update `exploration_log.md` + `DIGEST.md` in same PR).
- **New module / Phase M PR** → [knowledge/research/memory-architecture-design-2026-04-26.md](./knowledge/research/memory-architecture-design-2026-04-26.md) §9 roadmap + [knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md](./knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md).
- **Research-briefing note** → [knowledge/prompts/research-briefing.md](./knowledge/prompts/research-briefing.md), then [knowledge/research/_template.md](./knowledge/research/_template.md).
- **Edit `exploration_log.md`** → schema in [knowledge/README.md](./knowledge/README.md) §`trace/`.
- **Any PR** → [knowledge/skills/pr-creation/SKILL.md](./knowledge/skills/pr-creation/SKILL.md) (5-intent classifier + PR Checklist + anti-shallow-fix gate). Load via [AGENTS.md §Loadable skills](./AGENTS.md#loadable-skills).
- **Relocate / rename a module?** → grep [knowledge/anti-patterns/README.md](./knowledge/anti-patterns/README.md) first; AP-001 is the canonical path-bypass trap.
- **Specific number / date / quote** → primary source from `source:` frontmatter, NOT a summary. Per [AGENTS.md §Query Routing](./AGENTS.md#query-routing).

# STOP HERE UNLESS SPECIFICALLY ROUTED.
---

## § BY-DEMAND INDEX

### Inner-loop runtime (`src/fa/inner_loop/`)

Materialised ADR-7 + ADR-8 runtime. Smoke: `fa inner-loop-smoke --workspace . --input README.md`. Tests: `tests/test_inner_loop_*.py` + `tests/test_hook_registry.py` + `tests/test_verifier.py` + `tests/test_cost_guardian.py` + `tests/test_roles.py`.

- `src/fa/inner_loop/registry.py` — `ToolRegistry` + `ToolSpec` / `ToolResult` / `ToolCall` dataclasses; JSON-Schema validation per ADR-7 §5.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/loop.py` — deterministic `run_session()` driver; `max_iterations` from `RuntimeLimits` (default 6, ADR-7 §Amendment 2026-05-20).
- `src/fa/inner_loop/coder_loop.py` — M-8 LLM-driven `drive_session()`; bridges `ProviderChain` and `run_session`; returns `SessionOutcome`.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/prompt.py` — M-8 A-bucket residue: `CODER_SYSTEM_PROMPT` + `render_tool_specs()` (OpenAI function-tool shape) + `build_system_message()`.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/runtime_limits.py` — `RuntimeLimits` config loader from `~/.fa/config.yaml`; all knob defaults anchored.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/base.py` — `HookRegistry`, `GuardMiddleware` / `ObserverMiddleware`, `Decision`, lifecycle dispatch. ADR-8 contract.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/builtin.py` — `PauseGuard`, `CapabilityGuard`, `SandboxHook`, `ApprovalHook`, `AuditHook`, `VerifierObserver`, `LearningObserver` (R-8 canon writer).
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/loop_guard.py` — R-2 `LoopGuard`: identical-call repeat + same-path thrash detectors over sliding window.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/blockers.py` — R-4 `RateLimitBlocker` / `LockfileBlocker` / `AuthExpiredBlocker`; gate + observe at `BEFORE/AFTER_TOOL_EXEC`.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/recovery_observers.py` — R-3 `RecoveryActionObserver` + `AttemptHistoryObserver`; wired at `AFTER_TOOL_EXEC`.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/intent_guard.py` — M-7 `IntentGuard(GuardMiddleware)` on `BEFORE_TOOL_EXEC`; reuses `fa.hygiene.pr_intent.classify_intent` + `validate_commit_msg` (ADR-10 I-1: one validator across git hook / middleware / `pr.prepare`). Requires a trusted current-session draft for mutating `fs.write_file` and for `fs.run_bash` commands the AST analyzer classifies as writing/opaque; allow-on-no-draft / allow-on-git-fail. Wired into `fa run` bootstrap (`cli.py` `_cmd_run`).
- `src/fa/inner_loop/recovery/` — pure data layer for R-3 + R-6: `classify.py` (deterministic error→action table), `attempt_history.py` (sliding-window JSON writer).
- `src/fa/inner_loop/bash_intent.py` — AST-backed (`bashlex`) `fs.run_bash` effect analyzer; classifies a command into READ_ONLY / VERIFY_ONLY / INDEX_WRITE / REPO_WRITE / OPAQUE_EXEC so `IntentGuard` can apply the draft-first contract to shell writes. Fails closed to OPAQUE_EXEC.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/pr_draft.py` — `PrDraftStore`: current-session trusted PR-draft store at `~/.fa/state/runs/<run_id>/pr_draft.md`; atomic write + content-digest provenance so `IntentGuard` trusts only drafts produced this run (rejects stale / externally-fabricated files).
- `src/fa/inner_loop/state.py` — `SessionState` + `EventLog`; ADR-7 §7 JSONL schema.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/tools/` — `build_baseline_registry()` (three M-1 tools: `fs.read_file`, `fs.write_file`, `fs.run_bash`) + `build_prepare_pr_tool()` (`pr.prepare` — M-7 §Q-N producer of `~/.fa/state/runs/<run_id>/pr_draft.md`).
- `src/fa/verifier/` — R-5 DSV module: `verify_action(contract, events)` against `verifiers/*.yaml` contracts.
- `verifiers/` — vendored DSV contracts for M-1 tools + `edit_file.yaml`.

### Providers (`src/fa/providers/`)

ADR-9 implementation. Per-role explicit provider chain with cooldown in `~/.fa/models.yaml`.

- `src/fa/providers/base.py` — `ProviderAdapter` ABC + `ResponseInfo` canonical response.
- `src/fa/providers/chain.py` — `ProviderChain` ordered fallback; `chain_from_mapping()` YAML→chain constructor with family normalization.
- `src/fa/providers/openai_compat.py` — `OpenAICompatProvider`; covers OpenRouter / Fireworks / NVIDIA / Groq / etc.
- `src/fa/providers/anthropic.py` — `AnthropicProvider`; system-as-separate-field + tool-use content blocks.
- `src/fa/providers/config.py` — `ModelsConfig` + `load_models_config()` from `~/.fa/models.yaml`; enforces ADR-2 eval-disjoint rule.
- `src/fa/providers/errors.py` — 6 typed errors (`ConfigurationError` through `ProviderChainExhaustedError`).
- `src/fa/providers/registry.py` — `ProviderSpec` + adapter factory registry.
- `src/fa/providers/transport.py` — M-8 `UrllibTransport`: stdlib `Transport` impl via `urllib.request`; no third-party dep.

### Observability (`src/fa/observability/`)

- `src/fa/observability/cost_guardian.py` — R-45 `CostGuardian` `GuardMiddleware`; tri-mode cost budget (unbounded / observe-only / hard cap).
- `src/fa/observability/cost_table.py` — model+provider price lookup; pricing-miss → `cost_usd: null` + warning.

### Sandbox (`src/fa/sandbox/`)

ADR-6 bash sandbox gate (Wave-1). Three-layer pipeline: pattern classifier → per-command validators → path containment.

- `src/fa/sandbox/classifier.py` — 5-category bash command classifier (`READ_ONLY` / `GIT_WRITE` / `PACKAGE_INSTALL` / `DANGEROUS` / `GENERAL_WRITE`).
- `src/fa/sandbox/validators.py` — per-command deny rules (`rm` / `chmod` / `git`).
- `src/fa/sandbox/path_containment.py` — symlink-resolved workspace-root containment check.
- `src/fa/sandbox/bash_gate.py` — `evaluate_bash()` single entry point; AND-ed with ADR-6 §Policy file at dispatcher.

### Role-config helpers (`src/fa/roles.py`)

- `src/fa/roles.py` — `extract_family(slug)` regex slug-to-family inference; `check_eval_disjoint()` enforces ADR-2 §Amendment 2026-05-20. R-19.

### Config (`src/fa/config.py`)

- `src/fa/config.py` — `Capabilities` dataclass (5 deny-by-default flags per ADR-6 §Amendment 2026-05-20) + YAML parser from `~/.fa/config.yaml`.

### Hygiene (`src/fa/hygiene/`)

PR-intent classifier + git-hook installer (M-6 / M-7 producer side). Shared validator powers the git hook, the harness `IntentGuard`, and the `pr.prepare` tool (ADR-10 I-1 single-source-of-truth).

- `src/fa/hygiene/pr_intent.py` — `validate_commit_msg()` + `HEADER_*` constants + 5-intent classifier; the one validator all three PR-intent seats call.
- `src/fa/hygiene/hooks/install.py` — `install_hooks()` symlinks the `prepare-commit-msg` / `commit-msg` scripts into `.git/hooks/`. Tested by `tests/test_hygiene_hooks_install.py`.
- `src/fa/hygiene/tokens.py` / `discover.py` / `suggestions.py` — token classification, record discovery, and hygiene suggestions helpers.

### Tooling & CI (`pyproject.toml`, `.pylintrc-tests`, `.github/workflows/`)

Quality gates run in CI. `make check` (ruff + mypy + pytest) mirrors CI locally; thresholds live in `pyproject.toml`, not in workflow YAML.

- `pyproject.toml` — single config surface: ruff, mypy (strict), pytest, `[tool.coverage.*]` (branch coverage + `fail_under = 90`), `[tool.pylint.*]` (cosmetic checks disabled, `duplicate-code` kept ON for src, `fail-under = 9.0`), `[tool.mutmut]`.
- `.pylintrc-tests` — tests-only pylint profile; relaxes pytest idioms (fixtures, test-double args, protected-access) and disables `duplicate-code` / `C1803` for `tests/` (the src profile keeps them ON). Two-pass lint because pylint does not auto-merge per-directory rc.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — ruff + mypy + pytest (coverage-gated at 90%); uploads `coverage.xml` for GitHub Code coverage analysis.
- `.github/workflows/pylint.yml` — two-pass pylint (src strict w/ duplicate-code; tests relaxed via `.pylintrc-tests`). Installs `.[dev]` so imports resolve (no spurious `E0401`).
- `.github/workflows/tests.yml` — mutation testing (`mutmut`) only; manual + weekly, advisory (not a PR gate). Guards against tests that pass but assert nothing.

### Project knowledge (`knowledge/`)

- [knowledge/glossary.md](./knowledge/glossary.md): Term definitions. Listed in §MUST READ FIRST.
- [knowledge/README.md](./knowledge/README.md): Knowledge folder layout, schemas for `trace/` files, file placement rules.
- [knowledge/project-overview.md](./knowledge/project-overview.md): Four-pillar goal, minimalism-first principle, v0.1 scope, three-stage evolution. Listed in §MUST READ FIRST.
- [knowledge/BACKLOG.md](./knowledge/BACKLOG.md) (Large): Deferred ideas with unblock triggers. Active milestones: M-6 (PR B), M-7 (PR C). Closed: M-1..M-5, I-9.
- [knowledge/MAINTENANCE.md](./knowledge/MAINTENANCE.md): Cross-reference cascade rules — touch-lists for archiving, amending, superseding, adding files. Companion to `BACKLOG.md`.
- [knowledge/architecture.md](./knowledge/architecture.md): High-level architecture overview (inner-loop runtime, provider chain, hook families, sandbox + draft-first enforcement, ADR-10 invariants) with Mermaid diagram.

### ADRs (`knowledge/adr/`)

- [knowledge/adr/README.md](./knowledge/adr/README.md): ADR process and ordered index.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-template.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-template.md): Template for new ADRs.
- [knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md](./knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md): One-paragraph cheat-sheet per ADR + amendments. Listed in §MUST READ FIRST.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-1-v01-use-case-scope.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-1-v01-use-case-scope.md): v0.1 ships UC1 + UC3; UC4 deferred; UC5 eval-harness expanded.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-2-llm-tiering.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-2-llm-tiering.md): Static role routing (Planner/Coder/Debug/Eval); no auto-escalation; eval-family disjoint rule.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-3-memory-architecture-variant.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-3-memory-architecture-variant.md): Variant A "Mechanical Wiki" — filesystem-canonical Markdown + SQLite FTS5 BM25.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-4-storage-backend.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-4-storage-backend.md): SQLite FTS5 at `~/.fa/state/index.sqlite`; no vector store in v0.1.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-5-chunker-tool.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-5-chunker-tool.md): universal-ctags (code) + markdown-it-py (prose); tree-sitter explicit non-goal.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-6-tool-sandbox-allow-list.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-6-tool-sandbox-allow-list.md): Path allow-list + capability flags + bash sandbox gate. No `run_command` in v0.1.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-7-inner-loop-tool-registry.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-7-inner-loop-tool-registry.md) (Large): Inner-loop boundary contract: ToolSpec registry, 5-tool catalog, edit shapes, hook pipeline, event schema.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-8-hook-registry.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-8-hook-registry.md): HookRegistry lifecycle contract: Guard/Observer middleware, lifecycle points, first-deny short-circuit.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-9-llm-provider-client.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-9-llm-provider-client.md): Per-role explicit provider chain with per-(provider,slug) cooldown. T-2 driver + T-4 loader landed.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-10-deterministic-harness-invariants.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-10-deterministic-harness-invariants.md): Five named invariants (I-1..I-5) every harness component must satisfy. Proposed 2026-05-25. Companion §1.2.5 in project-overview.md.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-11-authoring-guardrails.md](./knowledge/adr/ADR-11-authoring-guardrails.md): Authoring-time guardrails — two-tier TCB (frozen stdlib Level-0 kernel + allowlisted Level-1 rules), `ADR-11-I1..I8` slate, LLM-as-Untrusted-Compiler threat model. Proposed 2026-06-01.

### Anti-pattern catalog (`knowledge/anti-patterns/`)

- [knowledge/anti-patterns/README.md](./knowledge/anti-patterns/README.md): Entry schema + Layer-1/2/3 detection model. 3 entries so far.
- [knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-001-spec-bypassing-workaround.md](./knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-001-spec-bypassing-workaround.md): Path-relocation workaround to silence `git status` — wrong shape + cost-asymmetry trap.
- [knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-002-stale-routing-index-counts.md](./knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-002-stale-routing-index-counts.md): llms.txt line-count drift. First RELAX dogfood of the Level-2 CLASS sub-classifier (now in pr-creation skill).
- [knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-003-shallow-fix-no-mechanism.md](./knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-003-shallow-fix-no-mechanism.md): FIX PR without `DETERMINISTIC MECHANISM:` citation — synthetic worked-history placeholder.

### Exploration log (`knowledge/trace/`)

- [knowledge/trace/exploration_log.md](./knowledge/trace/exploration_log.md) (Large): Alternatives rejected at decision time — Q-1..Q-15 with Chosen/Rejected/Lesson/Coupling. Cheap-read overlay for ADR rationale.

### Prompts (`knowledge/prompts/`)

- [knowledge/prompts/README.md](./knowledge/prompts/README.md): Prompts folder index.
- [knowledge/prompts/RESOLVER.md](./knowledge/prompts/RESOLVER.md): Intent → prompt-template dispatch table.
- [knowledge/prompts/prompting.md](./knowledge/prompts/prompting.md): Prompting strategy + T1–T5 templates + anti-patterns; the fallback target RESOLVER.md T2–T5 rows cite (backlog I-5).
- [knowledge/prompts/architect-fa.md](./knowledge/prompts/architect-fa.md): Planner system prompt — bounded recon, subtraction-check, scope declaration.
- [knowledge/prompts/architect-fa-compact.md](./knowledge/prompts/architect-fa-compact.md): Compact variant of architect-fa for mid-tier models.
- [knowledge/prompts/coder-recovery.md](./knowledge/prompts/coder-recovery.md): Coder error-recovery prompt fragment, reads `attempt_history.json`.
- [knowledge/prompts/handoff-summarizer.md](./knowledge/prompts/handoff-summarizer.md): Session-handoff / compaction prompt. Verbatim identifier preservation + 5-block structure. For inner-loop compaction and session-end notes, not for `HANDOFF.md`.
- [knowledge/prompts/research-briefing.md](./knowledge/prompts/research-briefing.md): Research-briefing workflow prompt. Stage 1 goal_lens elicitation.

### Skills (`knowledge/skills/`)

- [knowledge/skills/README.md](./knowledge/skills/README.md): Skill directory layout, template, skill-vs-prompt-vs-rule distinction.
- [knowledge/skills/pr-creation/SKILL.md](./knowledge/skills/pr-creation/SKILL.md): Canonical PR-creation rulebook: 5-intent classifier, anti-shallow-fix gate, PR Checklist rules 1-10. Load trigger: before any PR.
- [knowledge/skills/repo-audit/SKILL.md](./knowledge/skills/repo-audit/SKILL.md): 7-phase audit workflow (orientation → inventory → cross-reference → invariants → contradiction sweep → demotion → report). Load trigger: critical structure/doc review.

### Research (`knowledge/research/`)

> **Convention.** Each active note has a `§0 Decision Briefing` — read that before deciding to load the full note. Rows below carry only the goal-sentence and bucket.

- [knowledge/research/_template.md](./knowledge/research/_template.md): Research note template with §0 Decision Briefing pre-filled.
- [knowledge/research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md): [goal: Harness architecture survey — §10 is the ADR-7 contract sketch input.]
- [knowledge/research/cutting-edge-agent-research-radar-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/cutting-edge-agent-research-radar-2026-05.md): [goal: Survey cutting-edge agent papers for FA-applicable patterns.]
- [knowledge/research/semi-autonomous-agents-cross-reference-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/semi-autonomous-agents-cross-reference-2026-05.md) (Large): [goal: Cross-reference semi-autonomous agent designs against FA scope.]
- [knowledge/research/memory-architecture-design-2026-04-26.md](./knowledge/research/memory-architecture-design-2026-04-26.md): [goal: Memory architecture options → ADR-3 + ADR-4 input. §9 roadmap.]
- [knowledge/research/how-to-build-an-agent-ampcode-2026-04.md](./knowledge/research/how-to-build-an-agent-ampcode-2026-04.md): [goal: Extract patterns from Amp/Ampcode for FA inner-loop design.]
- [knowledge/research/latent-verifier-evolve-research-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/latent-verifier-evolve-research-2026-05.md): [goal: Verifier evolution and latent-space validation patterns for FA eval.]
- [knowledge/research/ara-protocol-cross-reference-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/ara-protocol-cross-reference-2026-05.md): [goal: Cross-reference Ara protocol (arXiv:2604.24658v2) against ADR-1..6. 10 R-Ns.]
- [knowledge/research/chat-cross-reference-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/chat-cross-reference-2026-05.md): [goal: Cross-reference chat transcripts for overlooked FA-applicable patterns.]
- [knowledge/research/dpc-messenger-inspiration-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/dpc-messenger-inspiration-2026-05.md): [goal: Extract from dpc-messenger (TypeScript agent, LoopState + guards) patterns for FA inner-loop.]
- [knowledge/research/soviet-code-inspiration-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/soviet-code-inspiration-2026-05.md): [goal: Extract from soviet-code (Python agent, prompt-first) anti-patterns and FA-applicable patterns.]
- [knowledge/research/gortex-aperant-inspiration-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/gortex-aperant-inspiration-2026-05.md): [goal: Extract from gortex (Go code-intel daemon) and Aperant (Electron planner/coder/qa) patterns for FA.]
- [knowledge/research/kronos-agent-os-inspiration-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/kronos-agent-os-inspiration-2026-05.md): [goal: Extract from kronos-agent-os (Python agent OS, SQLite-canonical) patterns for FA. 6 R-Ns.]
- [knowledge/research/borrow-roadmap-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/borrow-roadmap-2026-05.md) (Large): [goal: Consolidate 7 research notes into actionable 30 R-N borrow list across 5 implementation waves.] **Canonical source for all R-N citations added 2026-05-20.**
- [knowledge/research/bootstrap-cost-baseline-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/bootstrap-cost-baseline-2026-05.md): [goal: First quantitative bootstrap-cost baseline — 7 sessions, 6-file irreducible core.] Measurement note, not research-briefing.
- [knowledge/research/provider-client-survey-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/provider-client-survey-2026-05.md): [goal: Audit 8 OSS LLM provider clients to validate ADR-9 Option D + α.] Companion to ADR-9.
- [knowledge/research/correlated-llm-errors-and-ensembling-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/correlated-llm-errors-and-ensembling-2026-05.md) (Large): [goal: Cross-reference 4 arxiv papers on correlated LLM errors against ADR-1/2/7. 9 R-Ns across §0 + §10.]
- [knowledge/research/fa-abc-synthesis-deep-dive-2026-05.md](./knowledge/research/fa-abc-synthesis-deep-dive-2026-05.md) (Large): [goal: 9-repo determinism-pattern deep-dive → ADR-10 invariants I-1..I-5.] Jump-table use: read §0+§3+§4+§6 (~5k tokens), open §1.x only for cited pattern IDs.
